spec.desc: Container translation: Container_ID = VLCN >> CPC_BitCount, where the shift = CPC.bit_length() = 15 for 4 KiB clusters and 11 for 64 KiB clusters (E18-corrected: prior 14/10 omitted the +1).
spec.evidence_quote: structure_reference.md L286: "shift = CPC.bit_length() // 15 for 4 KiB clusters, 11 for 64 KiB" and L246: "0x18 | 4 | Clusters per container (CPC) | u32 | 16,384 (4 KiB) or 1,024 (64 KiB) | CONFIRMED"
spec.explanation: The asserted shift is not an independent constant: shift = CPC.bit_length(), so it is fully and uniquely determined by CPC. The `cpc` probe tests CPC == 16384 (4K) / 1024 (64K) read from value+0x18, and 16384.bit_length()=15 while 1024.bit_length()=11 exactly reproduce the two asserted shift values per cluster size. A wrong CPC would yield a wrong shift, so this is not a tautology. Axis=cluster because 4K vs 64K give different CPC and thus different shift (15 vs 11). Driver corroboration: GetContainerIdFromRealRange at 0x1400b3e64 shifts by (field_0x50 + 1) = log2(CPC)+1 = CPC.bit_length() (errata E18).

spec.desc: Container translation: offset-in-container CN = VLCN & (CPC - 1), i.e. the low-order mask equals clusters-per-container minus one (16383 for 4K / 1023 for 64K).
spec.evidence_quote: structure_reference.md L287: "mask = CPC - 1" and L289: "offset_in_container = vlcn & mask"; CPC fixed by L246: "0x18 | 4 | Clusters per container (CPC) | u32 | 16,384 (4 KiB) or 1,024 (64 KiB) | CONFIRMED"
spec.explanation: The asserted mask is mask = CPC - 1, fully determined by CPC. The `cpc` probe verifies CPC == 16384 (4K) / 1024 (64K) at value+0x18; from a verified CPC the mask 0x3FFF (4K) / 0x3FF (64K) follows deterministically. A wrong CPC would give a wrong mask, so the probe specifically tests the asserted value rather than passing regardless. Axis=cluster because the mask differs between 4K (16383) and 64K (1023). Structure_reference L296-298 note bit log2(CPC) is always zero in valid container LCNs, so CPC-1 is the correct mask despite the bit_length-based shift.

spec.desc: Deep CT row structure: CPC universally at value+0x18 (16384/1024); 160-byte row (4K/CRC64) vs 224-byte row (64K or SHA256) with 128-byte extended integrity area; CSC at value[len-16].
spec.explanation: The single load-bearing on-disk constant in this claim is the CPC field at value+0x18, which the cpc probe verifies (16384/1024); structure_reference states 'CPC always at value+0x18' and forefst _parse_ct_page (forefst.py:338) reads le32(vd, 0x18) for CPC and le64(vd, len(vd)-16) for the physical start. The cpc probe tests the CPC-at-0x18 invariant. The row-size differentiation (160 vs 224) and the 128-byte extended integrity area are layout assertions with no constant value at a fixed offset, so they are split out into needs_probe; a constant probe cannot test 'row length is 160 or 224' nor a per-image variable physical-start value.
spec.evidence_quote: **Universal parsing rule:** CPC always at value+0x18; physical start always at value[len-16].

spec.desc: VBR offset 0x20 (u32, 4 bytes) = bytes per sector = 0x200 (512), constant on all images
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x20 | 4 | Bytes per sector | u32 | 512 (always) | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: The claim asserts a flat constant: the VBR 'Bytes per sector' field at offset 0x20 (decimal 32), 4 bytes wide, equals 512 = 0x200. structure_reference.md line 22 fixes the offset (0x20), size (u32 / 4 bytes), and value (512 'always'), marked CONFIRMED. The probe vbr_int(off=32, size=4, expected=512) reads exactly that field and asserts exactly that value, so it FAILs if any image deviates and is not a tautology. static_notes corroborate: 'uint32 at 0x20; validated range 0x200-0x1000; stored in VCB+0x114' and 'All images show 0x200'. axis=none because it is an invariant that does not vary by cluster size, version, or checksum.

spec.desc: CHKP major ReFS version field at offset 0x54 (u16) is always the constant 3 across every image.
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x54 | 2 | Major version | u16 | Always 3 | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md A.4 Checkpoint (CHKP) table line 162 fixes the field: offset 0x54 (decimal 84), size 2 bytes (u16), 'Major version', value 'Always 3', status CONFIRMED. The claim asserts a true CONSTANT (major version = 3 on every image — the CHKP major must equal the ReFS major, which has only ever been 3 for ReFS 3.4–3.14+Insider; minor is what varies, at 0x56). chkp_int reads the newest CHKP page u16 at off=0x54 and tests it == 3, exactly the asserted value, so a wrong major (e.g. 0/2) FAILs — not a tautology. parse_chkp (forefst.py:288) is the fn that seeks the CHKP page and parses its header fields. raw_notes for FS_CHKP_002 confirm 'always 3 across all images' and 'Major version matches VBR: 3 in all images'.

spec.desc: CHKP+0x90 (u32) root/pointer count == 13 (13 global root tables)
spec.explanation: Claim asserts a CONSTANT count of 13 global root tables. structure_reference.md A.4 lists CHKP+0x90 (offset 144 decimal) size 4 (u32) = Root count, 'Always 13'. forefst parse_chkp reads root_count = le32(raw,0x90). chkp_int(0x90,4,13) tests the exact asserted value 13; fails if the count differs, so not a tautology. (CSV labels the offset range 0x90-0x94; that is the same u32 at 0x90.)
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x90 | 4 | Root count | u32 | Always 13 | CONFIRMED |

spec.desc: CHKP+0x8C (u32) max root capacity == 0x20 (32) on all versions/configs
spec.explanation: Claim asserts the CONSTANT 0x20 (32) for max root capacity (MS_CHECKPOINT_MAX_ROOTS) at CHKP+0x8C, distinct from the live root count (13) at 0x90. structure_reference.md A.4 gives CHKP+0x8C (offset 140 decimal) size 4 (u32) = Max root capacity, 'Always 0x20 (32)'. chkp_int(0x8C,4,32) tests the exact asserted constant; fails if not 32, so not a tautology.
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x8C | 4 | Max root capacity | u32 | Always 0x20 (32) | CONFIRMED |

spec.desc: CHKP+0x7C 12-byte reserved field is always zero (Prade 'table refs offset/count/unknown buffer' all zero)
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x7C | 12 | Reserved | bytes | Always zero | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md A.4 line 170 fixes CHKP+0x7C as a 12-byte reserved field that is always zero on all images. chkp_int reads at most 8 bytes, so this spec asserts the first 8 reserved bytes (0x7C..0x83) == 0 (non-tautological: any nonzero byte there FAILs). The trailing 4 bytes (0x84..0x87) cannot be covered by the same probe and are flagged in needs_probe so the full 12-byte zero claim is completely audited.

spec.desc: VBR 0x00-0x02: 3-byte jump instruction is zeros (00 00 00, ReFS not bootable)
spec.explanation: The jump field is 3 zero bytes at 0x00-0x02; the menu has no 3-byte int size, so vbr_int(0,2,0) asserts bytes 0x00-0x01 == 0 (the first two of the three jump bytes), which fails if any non-zero jump opcode is present. Byte 0x02 cannot be covered by a single int probe without overlapping the 'R' of 'ReFS' at 0x03. This is a partial test of the constant; flagged below as well for full 3-byte coverage.
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x00 | 3 | Jump instruction | bytes | `00 00 00` (no x86 jump; ReFS not bootable except Insider) | CONFIRMED |

spec.desc: VBR 0x10-0x14 holds the ASCII signature 'FSRS'
spec.explanation: The claim asserts a fixed 4-byte ASCII signature 'FSRS' at VBR+0x10. structure_reference.md A.1 fixes offset 0x10 size 4 = "FSRS". As a little-endian u32 the bytes 46 53 52 53 ('F','S','R','S') = 0x53525346 = 1397904198. vbr_int(0x10=16, size=4, expected=1397904198) directly tests the asserted constant; any other 4 bytes FAIL.
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x10 | 4 | FSRS identifier | ASCII | `"FSRS"` signature | CONFIRMED |

spec.desc: VBR offset 0x14 (u16) is the VBR structure size, constant 0x200 (512).
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x14 | 2 | VBR size | u16 | 0x0200 (512) | ENRICHED |
spec.explanation: Constant. structure_reference.md A.1 fixes offset 0x14, size 2 (u16) == 0x0200 = 512. vbr_int(20,2,512) tests exactly the asserted constant 0x200.

spec.desc: VBR offset 0x40 (u64) bytes-per-container is the invariant constant 0x04000000 (64 MiB).
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x40 | 8 | Bytes per container | u64 | 0x04000000 (64 MiB, invariant) | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: Constant invariant. structure_reference.md A.1 fixes offset 0x40, size 8 (u64) == 0x04000000 = 67108864. vbr_u64(64,67108864) tests exactly the asserted 64 MiB container size, regardless of 4K/64K cluster size (it is the same byte value on all images per the 'invariant' note).

spec.desc: Superblock version/recency field (0x68, u64) is constant 1 on all images.
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x68 | 8 | Superblock version | u64 | Always 1 (not the ReFS format version) | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: Constant. structure_reference.md A.3 fixes SUPB offset 0x68 (u64) == 1 ('Always 1'). The asserted value is a flat constant. NOTE: the probe menu has vbr_int/chkp_int but NO supb_int probe — vbr_int reads the VBR, not the SUPB at LCN 0x1E, so it CANNOT test this field. There is no menu probe that reads the Superblock. See needs_probe; this spec is provided only to record the verified offset/value.

spec.desc: Superblock checkpoint-refs offset field (0x70, u32) is typically 0xC0.
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x70 | 4 | Checkpoint refs offset | u32 | Typically 0xC0 | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: Constant 0xC0 = 192. structure_reference.md A.3 fixes SUPB offset 0x70 (u32) == 0xC0 ('Typically'). NOTE: 'Typically' is weaker than 'Always', and the menu has NO supb_int probe (vbr_int reads the VBR sector, not the SUPB at LCN 0x1E). This field cannot be tested by any menu probe. Recorded in needs_probe.

spec.desc: Superblock checkpoint-refs count field (0x74, u32) is constant 2 (A/B checkpoint scheme).
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x74 | 4 | Checkpoint refs count | u32 | Always 2 | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: Constant. structure_reference.md A.3 fixes SUPB offset 0x74 (u32) == 2 ('Always 2'). NOTE: no supb_int menu probe exists (vbr_int reads the VBR sector, not SUPB at LCN 0x1E). Cannot be tested by a menu probe; recorded in needs_probe.

spec.desc: Page reference offset 0x24 (u32) checksum-data length: 4 (CRC32-C) / 8 (CRC64) / 32 (SHA-256).
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x24 | 4 | Checksum data length | u32 | 4 (CRC32-C), 8 (CRC64), 32 (SHA-256) | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: The checksum-data length is a checksum-type-dependent constant, NOT a single flat value. structure_reference.md D.1 fixes page-reference offset 0x24 (u32) == 8 for CRC64. For CRC64 images the value is the constant 8; scope with applicability checksum=CRC64. CAUTION: page_const per the menu reads an 'OT-root MSB+ page u32 at off' (a page header), whereas 0x24 here is inside a Page Reference embedded in a parent page, not at page-header offset 0x24. The menu page_const offset is page-header-relative, so it likely does NOT address a page-reference's +0x24. This mismatch means no menu probe cleanly tests the page-reference checksum length; see needs_probe.

spec.desc: CHKP+0x88 (u32) data area end offset is 0x380 on v3.14 (= 0x110 + 13*0x30)
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x88 | 4 | Data area end offset | u32 | 0x680 (v3.4), 0x380 (v3.14) | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md A.4 line 171 fixes CHKP+0x88 (136 decimal) as a u32 data-area-end constant that is version-dependent: 0x380 (896) on v3.14, 0x680 on v3.4. This spec tests the v3.14 value 0x380 (applicability version_min 3.14); the v3.4 value 0x680 requires a separate spec because chkp_int can assert only one constant. The value is a genuine constant per version (0x110 + 13*0x30 = 0x380), so chkp_int is non-tautological.

spec.desc: CHKP+0x50 (u32) version echo == packed 0x000E0003 on native/upgraded Win11 v3.14
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x50 | 4 | Version echo | u32 | packed minor<<16 | major on native v3.10+; 0 on upgraded/legacy | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md A.4 fixes CHKP+0x50 as a 4-byte version echo packed as (minor<<16)|major. For v3.14 this is 0x000E0003 = (14<<16)|3 = 917507, a constant within the v3.14 group. refsanalysis.py reads ver_echo via le16(raw,0x50)/le16(raw,0x52). chkp_int(off=0x50=80, size=4, expected=0x000E0003=917507) tests the exact packed v3.14 value. The Win10/v3.4 value (0x00000000) is the legacy default and is asserted separately — see needs_probe. NOTE: structure_reference labels 0x50 'native v3.10+', so this Win11-3.14 constant probe applies to v3.14 images; the upgraded-vs-legacy distinction at 0x50 is version-scoped.

spec.desc: Root #0 (slot 0 of the CHKP root pointer list) is the Object ID Table, table ID 0x02
spec.evidence_quote: | 0 | Object ID Table | 0x02 | 0xe030 | Virtual | #5 | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md Subtable A.4b fixes root index 0 -> table ID 0x02. This is a CONSTANT mapping, but NO menu probe (vbr_int/chkp_int/page_const/cpc/si_*/absent) can read a table ID out of the root-pointer-list: the table ID lives inside the page reference at CHKP+0x94+idx*ref_size, with ref_size being version/checksum-dependent (0x68/0x30/0x48) AND the table ID embedded at a sub-offset within each page-reference structure, not at a flat CHKP byte offset. chkp_int only reads a flat CHKP offset and cannot resolve the indirect-root-list mode (flag 0x200). Mapped to cite as a placeholder; the real test is specified in needs_probe (chkp_root_table_id).

spec.desc: Root #1 (Medium Allocator) has table ID 0x21
spec.evidence_quote: | 1 | Medium Allocator | 0x21 | 0xe010 | Virtual | — | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md Subtable A.4b fixes root index 1 -> table ID 0x21. No flat-offset menu probe can read a root-pointer-list table ID (version-dependent ref_size + indirect-list mode + embedded sub-offset). Mapped to cite placeholder; real test specified in needs_probe (chkp_root_table_id).

spec.desc: Root #2 (Container Allocator) has table ID 0x20; uses virtual addressing (invalidates Prade real-addressing claim)
spec.evidence_quote: | 2 | Container Allocator | 0x20 | 0xe010 | Virtual | — | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md Subtable A.4b fixes root index 2 -> table ID 0x20 with Virtual addressing. No flat-offset menu probe can read a root-pointer-list table ID. The addressing-mode half of the claim (virtual vs Prade's real) is a translation-behavior test, not a constant read. Mapped to cite placeholder; real tests specified in needs_probe (chkp_root_table_id + root2_virtual_addressing).

spec.desc: Root #3 (Schema Table) has table ID 0x01
spec.evidence_quote: | 3 | Schema Table | 0x01 | 0xe060 | Virtual | #9 | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md Subtable A.4b fixes root index 3 -> table ID 0x01. No flat-offset menu probe can read a root-pointer-list table ID. Mapped to cite placeholder; real test specified in needs_probe (chkp_root_table_id).

spec.desc: Root #4 (Parent-Child Table) has table ID 0x03
spec.evidence_quote: | 4 | Parent-Child Table | 0x03 | 0xe040 | Virtual | — | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md Subtable A.4b fixes root index 4 -> table ID 0x03. No flat-offset menu probe can read a root-pointer-list table ID. Mapped to cite placeholder; real test specified in needs_probe (chkp_root_table_id).

spec.desc: Root #5 (Object ID Table duplicate) has table ID 0x04
spec.evidence_quote: | 5 | Object ID Table (duplicate) | 0x04 | 0xe030 | Virtual | — | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md Subtable A.4b fixes root index 5 -> table ID 0x04 (failover duplicate of root #0). No flat-offset menu probe can read a root-pointer-list table ID. Mapped to cite placeholder; real test specified in needs_probe (chkp_root_table_id).

spec.desc: Root #6 (Block Refcount Table) has table ID 0x05
spec.evidence_quote: | 6 | Block Refcount | 0x05 | 0xe0b0 | Virtual | — | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md Subtable A.4b fixes root index 6 -> table ID 0x05. No flat-offset menu probe can read a root-pointer-list table ID. Mapped to cite placeholder; real test specified in needs_probe (chkp_root_table_id).

spec.desc: Root #7 (Container Table) has table ID 0x0B; uses REAL LCNs (bootstrap exception, VLCN=PLCN)
spec.evidence_quote: | 7 | Container Table | 0x0B | 0xe0c0 | Real (physical) | #8 | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md Subtable A.4b fixes root index 7 -> table ID 0x0B with Real (physical) addressing, also noted in A.4: 'Roots 7, 8, and 12 use real (physical) LCNs as a bootstrap exception, confirmed by ValidateCheckpointRecord array {0xC, 7, 8}'. No flat-offset menu probe can read a root-pointer-list table ID, nor can any menu probe assert VLCN=PLCN. Mapped to cite placeholder; real tests in needs_probe (chkp_root_table_id + root7_real_addressing).

spec.desc: Root #8 (Container Table duplicate) has table ID 0x0C; uses REAL LCNs (VLCN=PLCN)
spec.evidence_quote: | 8 | Container Table (duplicate) | 0x0C | 0xe0c0 | Real (physical) | — | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md Subtable A.4b fixes root index 8 -> table ID 0x0C with Real (physical) addressing (failover duplicate of root #7). No flat-offset menu probe can read a root-pointer-list table ID or assert VLCN=PLCN. Mapped to cite placeholder; real tests in needs_probe (chkp_root_table_id + root8_real_addressing).

spec.desc: Root #9 (Schema Table duplicate) has table ID 0x06; uses virtual addressing
spec.evidence_quote: | 9 | Schema Table (duplicate) | 0x06 | 0xe060 | Virtual | — | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md Subtable A.4b fixes root index 9 -> table ID 0x06 with Virtual addressing. No flat-offset menu probe can read a root-pointer-list table ID. Mapped to cite placeholder; real test specified in needs_probe (chkp_root_table_id).

spec.desc: Root #10 (Container Index Table) has table ID 0x0E
spec.evidence_quote: | 10 | Container Index | 0x0E | 0xe100 | Virtual | — | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md Subtable A.4b fixes root index 10 -> table ID 0x0E. No flat-offset menu probe can read a root-pointer-list table ID. Mapped to cite placeholder; real test specified in needs_probe (chkp_root_table_id).

spec.desc: Root #11 (Integrity State Table) has table ID 0x0F; present regardless of integrity setting
spec.evidence_quote: | 11 | Integrity State | 0x0F | 0xe080 | Virtual | — | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md Subtable A.4b fixes root index 11 -> table ID 0x0F. No flat-offset menu probe can read a root-pointer-list table ID. Mapped to cite placeholder; real test specified in needs_probe (chkp_root_table_id).

spec.desc: Root #12 (Small Allocator Table) has table ID 0x22; uses REAL LCNs (bootstrap fallback, VLCN=PLCN)
spec.evidence_quote: | 12 | Small Allocator | 0x22 | 0xe010 | Real (physical) | — | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md Subtable A.4b fixes root index 12 -> table ID 0x22 with Real (physical) addressing; A.4 notes roots 7/8/12 use real LCNs as bootstrap exception. No flat-offset menu probe can read a root-pointer-list table ID or assert VLCN=PLCN. Mapped to cite placeholder; real tests in needs_probe (chkp_root_table_id + root12_real_addressing).

spec.desc: CHKP+0x5C (u32) page reference size is 0x30 on v3.14 CRC64 volumes
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x5C | 4 | Page reference size | u32 | 0x68 (v3.4), 0x30 (CRC64), 0x48 (SHA-256) | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md A.4 line 165 fixes CHKP+0x5C (92 decimal) as a u32 page-reference-size constant determined by version+checksum: 0x30 (48) for v3.14 CRC64, 0x68 for v3.4, 0x48 for SHA-256. forefst.parse_chkp reads desc_len = le32(raw, 0x5c) and uses it to stride the root pointer records. This spec asserts the CRC64 value 0x30 (applicability version_min 3.14, checksum CRC64); the 0x68 and 0x48 variants need separate specs. chkp_int is non-tautological: a wrong stride value FAILs.

spec.desc: CHKP+0x60 (u64) virtual clock body copy — per-image VARIABLE, mirrors header +0x10 and is monotonic
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x60 | 8 | Virtual clock (body copy) | u64 | Matches header at 0x10 | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md A.4 line 166 confirms CHKP+0x60 is a u64 virtual clock body copy that 'Matches header at 0x10' and increases monotonically (selection picks highest clock). There is NO single constant to assert — the value differs per image and grows over time — so no constant chkp_int probe applies (that would be a false FAIL/tautology). The real on-disk obligation is a consistency check (body 0x60 == header 0x10) and monotonic ordering; the menu has no such probe. Placed in needs_probe. (probe field set to cite as a placeholder; the binding claim is in needs_probe.)

spec.desc: CHKP+0x68 (u64) allocator clock — per-image VARIABLE, always <= virtual clock
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x68 | 8 | Allocator clock | u64 | <= virtual clock | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md A.4 line 167 fixes CHKP+0x68 as a u64 allocator clock whose only invariant is 'allocator clock <= virtual clock' (CSV: ranges 23..7200+, typically vclock-3..vclock-5). The value is per-image VARIABLE with no constant to assert, so no constant chkp_int probe is valid. The genuine test is the relation allocator_clock(0x68) <= virtual_clock(0x60), which the menu cannot express. Placed in needs_probe.

spec.desc: CHKP+0x70 (u64) oldest log record ref — VARIABLE compound (low32=log offset, high32=segment); identical in both CHKP copies
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x70 | 8 | Oldest log record ref | u64 | Low 32 bits = log offset, high 32 bits = segment | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md A.4 line 168 fixes CHKP+0x70 as a u64 compound oldest-log-record reference (low32=log area byte offset, high32=segment number; CSV values 0x39..0x138C8). It is per-image VARIABLE with no constant, so no constant chkp_int probe is valid. The real obligations are structural decomposition plausibility plus cross-copy equality (both CHKP copies identical at 0x70), neither expressible by the menu. Placed in needs_probe.

spec.desc: CHKP+0x56 (u16) minor ReFS version — VARIABLE per image (4 on Win10, 14 on Win11) and must equal the image's VBR minor version
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x56 | 2 | Minor version | u16 | 4, 7, 9, 10, or 14 | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md A.4 line 163 lists CHKP+0x56 minor version as one of {4,7,9,10,14} — there is NO single corpus-wide constant; it varies per image and the claim is that it MATCHES the image's VBR minor (CSV: 'Minor version matches VBR'). A constant chkp_int would FAIL on whichever versions are not the chosen constant, so it is invalid. The correct probe is per-image equality vs VBR minor (forefst reads h[0x56:0x58]); the menu lacks it. Placed in needs_probe.

spec.desc: CHKP+0x58 (u32) table-descriptor-end / self-descriptor offset — VARIABLE per version (0xD0 v3.4-3.10, 0xE0 v3.14, 0xE8 Insider)
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x58 | 4 | Table descriptor end | u32 | 0xD0 (v3.4–v3.10), 0xE0 (v3.14), 0xE8 (Insider) | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md A.4 line 164 fixes CHKP+0x58 as a u32 table-descriptor-end that is version-dependent (0xD0/0xE0/0xE8) — not a corpus constant. (The CSV row also muddles 0x58 vs 0x5C, an additional reason not to hard-code a single constant.) A constant chkp_int would falsely FAIL on the non-matching versions. The correct test is per-version equality keyed to the image's ReFS version; the menu has no such probe. Placed in needs_probe.

spec.desc: CHKP+0x78 (u32) flags full decomposition: bit 0x002 always set; composite values 0x002/0x602/0x682/0x7b2 by config
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x78 | 4 | CHKP flags | u32 | See Subtable A.4a | CONFIRMED | ; Observed composite values: 0x002 (v3.4/v3.7/v3.9), 0x082 (v3.10), 0x682 (v3.14 native), 0x602 (v3.14 upgraded), 0x07b2 (v3.14 dedup), 0x2682 (Insider).
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md A.4 line 169 + Subtable A.4a (lines 180-187) describe CHKP+0x78 as a u32 BITMASK with multiple observed composite values that vary by config (0x002/0x082/0x602/0x682/0x7b2/0x2682). The only flat invariant is bit 0x002 ALWAYS set, but chkp_int tests equality, not (val & 0x002)==0x002, so it cannot express the bitmask claim without falsely FAILing on the many composite values. forefst reads flags = le32(raw,0x78). The correct probe is a bitmask/enum-membership test; the menu lacks it. Placed in needs_probe.

spec.desc: Three volume states via CHKP+0x78 flags: original v3.4 (0x002), upgraded (0x602), native v3.14 (0x682); missing bit 0x080 distinguishes upgraded from native
spec.evidence_quote: Observed composite values: 0x002 (v3.4/v3.7/v3.9), 0x082 (v3.10), 0x682 (v3.14 native), 0x602 (v3.14 upgraded) ... | 7 | 0x0080 | Native format marker (not set on upgraded volumes) | v3.10 |
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md A.4a lines 181/187 establish the three forensic states by CHKP+0x78 flags (0x002 / 0x602 / 0x682) with native bit 0x080 distinguishing native v3.14 from upgraded. Per-image the flag equals ONE of three values (or 0x7b2 for dedup), so there is no single corpus constant for chkp_int, and the load-bearing test is enum-membership plus the 0x080 bit-clear discriminator. The menu has no enum/bitmask probe. Placed in needs_probe.

spec.desc: 3.4->3.14 upgrade transition: CHKP+0x78 flags change 0x002->0x602, CHKP+0x5C ref_size 0x68->0x30, tables relocated
spec.evidence_quote: | H.3 CHKP Flag Evolution ... | CHKP flags | 0x002 | 0x602 | NEW | ; | Compact page reference | CHKP+0x5C: 0x68 to 0x30 | ENRICHED |
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md H.3 (lines 1564/1627) documents the UPGRADE delta (flags 0x002->0x602, ref_size 0x68->0x30). This is a BEHAVIORAL before/after transition claim, not a single on-disk constant: proving it requires comparing the same volume pre- and post-upgrade (a paired-image probe). No single-image menu probe can assert a 'change'. Placed in needs_probe.

spec.desc: VBR 0x2A=checksum-algo selector, 0x2C-0x2F=volume flags, 0x30-0x37=reserved zero. Only the reserved-zero part (0x30-0x37) is a constant; the selector and flags are variable.
spec.explanation: This compound claim's only flat-constant component is the reserved field at VBR 0x30 (8 bytes), which structure_reference.md A.1 fixes as 'All zeros'. vbr_int at off=0x30(=48), size=8, expected=0 tests exactly that asserted constant (non-zero would FAIL), so it is not a tautology. The other two components (0x2A selector and 0x2C volume flags) are VARIABLE per image/version and have no single constant to assert (see needs_probe), so only the reserved-zero piece is mapped to a constant probe here.
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x30 | 8 | Reserved | bytes | All zeros | CONFIRMED |

spec.desc: VBR 0x18-0x20 total sector count (volume size in 512-byte sectors) — a VARIABLE field, no constant.
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md A.1 line 21 fixes offset 0x18 size 8 = 'Volume size in 512-byte sectors'. This is per-image VARIABLE (differs with every volume size); there is NO constant to assert. The vbr_int probe shown is a placeholder offset/size only — asserting any constant here would be a false test. The real check is a plausibility/consistency guard (nonzero, and sector_count*bytes_per_sector consistent with image size). See needs_probe.
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x18 | 8 | Total sector count | u64 | Volume size in 512-byte sectors | CONFIRMED |

spec.desc: VBR 0x24-0x28 sectors per cluster u32 == 8 (4K clusters) or 128 (64K clusters).
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md A.1 line 23 fixes offset 0x24 size 4 (u32) = '8 (4 KiB clusters) or 128 (64 KiB clusters)'. The value is NOT a flat constant — it is cluster-axis dependent (8 vs 128). I scope this spec to 4K images (applicability cluster=4096, expected 8); a SECOND spec is required for 64K (expected 128). vbr_int(0x24=36, size 4, expected 8) tests the asserted 4K value exactly. For 64K, author vbr_int(36,4,128) with applicability cluster=65536.
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x24 | 4 | Sectors per cluster | u32 | 8 (4 KiB clusters) or 128 (64 KiB clusters) | ENRICHED |

spec.desc: VBR 0x28-0x2A version (major.minor packed u16) — VARIABLE per image (0x0304..0x030E), no flat constant.
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md A.1 line 24 + Subtable A.1a fix offset 0x28 size 2 = packed version, but the value DIFFERS per image: 0x0304(3.4)/0x0307/0x0309/0x030A/0x030E. There is NO single constant — asserting one (e.g. 772=0x0304) would FAIL every non-3.4 image (false FAIL). This must be a per-image comparison against the image's known version, NOT a flat probe. The vbr_int shown is a placeholder; see needs_probe for the per-version shape.
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x28 | 2 | Version (major.minor) | u16 | See Subtable A.1a | CONFIRMED |

spec.desc: VBR 0x38-0x40 volume serial number u64 — VARIABLE, unique per volume, no constant.
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md A.1 line 28 fixes offset 0x38 size 8 = 'Volume serial number, Unique per volume'. This is VARIABLE per image; there is no constant to assert. Asserting any value is a false test. The meaningful check is plausibility (nonzero) plus the cross-structure consistency claim (preserved across upgrade) — see needs_probe. The vbr_int shown is offset/size placeholder only.
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x38 | 8 | Volume serial number | u64 | Unique per volume | CONFIRMED |

spec.desc: Page header LCN tuple 0x20-0x38: SUPB/CHKP use only slot0; LCN slots 1-3 (0x28/0x30/0x38) are zero on SUPB and CHKP.
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md A.2 lines 107-109 mark LCN slots 1/2/3 at +0x28/+0x30/+0x38 as 'MSB+ only', and A.2 plus this claim state they are zero on SUPB/CHKP. The asserted constant (zero) is real, but the page_const probe per the menu reads the OT-ROOT (MSB+) page — on an MSB+ page slots 1-3 are NON-zero (self+1..self+3), so page_const at 0x28 would NOT be zero. The claim is specifically about SUPB/CHKP pages, not MSB+. The menu has no probe that reads a SUPB/CHKP page slot; page_const targets the OT-root. Therefore this claim cannot be tested by page_const without a false FAIL. See needs_probe for a SUPB/CHKP-slot probe shape. The probe value shown is a placeholder.
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x28 | 8 | LCN slot 1 | u64 | MSB+ only: second cluster (self+1) | CONFIRMED |

spec.desc: Superblock located at cluster 30 (LCN 0x1E).
spec.explanation: structure_reference.md A.3 line 117 states SUPB is 'Always at cluster 30 (LCN 0x1E)'. This is a LOCATION claim, not an in-page byte field — none of the byte-offset probes (vbr_int/chkp_int/page_const) test where a page is found. The natural test is: the page at LCN 0x1E carries signature 'SUPB'. The menu has no 'page-at-LCN has signature' probe; the closest by intent is verifying the SUPB signature, but no probe reads an arbitrary LCN. See needs_probe for the SUPB-location/signature probe shape. cite is used as the menu's only non-byte-offset option but a real on-disk probe is required.
spec.evidence_quote: Always at cluster 30 (LCN 0x1E). Single cluster. Two backup copies near partition end (~total\_clusters - 14,339 and -14,338).

spec.desc: MsSetMinimumNewObjectId hardcodes 0x700 as the system/user OID boundary; user OIDs start at 0x701.
spec.evidence_quote: G.10 Key Functions: | MsSetMinimumNewObjectId | Hardcodes 0x700 as boundary; user OIDs start at 0x701 | CONFIRMED | (line 1481); also F.4: "User OIDs start at 0x701 (hardcoded via `MsSetMinimumNewObjectId`)." (line 1314)
spec.explanation: This is an E2 static-decompilation claim about a driver constant (0x700 boundary baked into MsSetMinimumNewObjectId), not a fixed value at a fixed on-disk offset. The probe menu has no Object-Table-row or driver-constant probe; the only fields it can read on disk are VBR/CHKP/page-header/$SI bytes. Proof is therefore the cited decomp source (G.10 / F.4 confirm 0x700). The on-disk corollary (every user OID >= 0x701) is a per-image variable distribution, not a constant the menu can assert, so cite is the correct mapping for the static assertion itself.

spec.desc: MsInitializeWellKnownObjectId builds a 128-bit SmsBigIdentifier from a 64-bit well-known OID, setting the upper 8 bytes to 0.
spec.evidence_quote: Subtable C.13a — 128-bit File ID Structure: | Upper 8 bytes | u64 | B+-tree table OID (directory's Object Table OID) | NEW | / | Lower 8 bytes | u64 | Sequential entry index ... | NEW | (lines 1024-1025). Object Table Key — 8 bytes: | 0x00 | 8 | Object ID (OID) | u64 | (line 363).
spec.explanation: E2 static-decompilation claim about a 12-byte helper's behavior (zeroing the high half of a 128-bit identifier). The Object Table key itself is a single u64 OID at key+0x00 (line 363), not a fixed-offset constant; the 128-bit File-ID upper/lower split (C.13a) is a structural layout, not a flat constant the menu can assert. No menu probe reads File-ID 128-bit composition. Proof is the cited decomp + C.13a layout.

spec.desc: MsGetObjectRecordPayload (46-byte helper) returns the LcnWithChecksum pointer from an Object Table row's value buffer at a header-derived offset.
spec.evidence_quote: B.3 Object Table Value — Compact Format: | 0x08 | 16 | Record header | format=2, key_offset=0x18, value_offset=0x30 | ENRICHED | and | 0x20 | 32 | 4 x u64 LCN slots | Page references for object's own B+-tree root | ENRICHED | (lines 370, 373).
spec.explanation: Pure E2 static-decompilation claim describing a driver helper that extracts the page-reference pointer from a row value at an offset derived from the row header. There is no fixed constant value to assert at a fixed VBR/CHKP/page offset; the payload location (value+0x20 LCN slots, reached via record header at +0x08) is structural, and build_object_map already reads value+0x20 confirming the layout. No menu probe targets OT row payload extraction, so proof = cited decomp + B.3 layout.

spec.desc: Volume label stored in OID 0x500 B+tree under key type 0x0510 (UTF-16LE)
spec.explanation: This is a B+tree key-type/layout claim inside OID 0x500 (Volume Information), not a fixed offset in VBR/CHKP/OT-root-page-header. The menu has no probe that walks the OID 0x500 B+tree and reads key type 0x0510, so there is no constant-offset probe; the on-disk fact (key 0x0510 = volume label) is documented in the reference. cite is the only honest mapping (no menu probe fits); the proof is the cited reference line. A real disk test would need a vinf_keytype probe (see needs_probe).
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x0510 | Variable | Volume label (UTF-16LE, null-terminated) | CONFIRMED |

spec.desc: RefsIsBootSectorOurs validates the 'ReFS' signature at VBR+0x03 (on-disk corollary: 'ReFS' present at 0x03)
spec.explanation: The static claim is about RefsIsBootSectorOurs checking 'ReFS' at +0x03. Its on-disk, corpus-testable corollary is that bytes at 0x03 spell 'ReFS' (52 65 46 53 = 0x53466552 = 1397122386). Testing the first 4 bytes of the 8-byte file-system-name field proves the validated signature is present on disk; a non-ReFS/FVE volume (e.g. '-FVE') fails.
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x03 | 8 | File system name | ASCII | `"ReFS\0\0\0\0"` | CONFIRMED |

spec.desc: ReFS deletion flow: RefsDeleteFile -> MsDeleteRow -> MsReparentFileToTrash -> CmsTrashTable::AddFileTable -> TrashCleanerWorkItemMethod -> DeleteFileTable
spec.explanation: This is a behavioral/control-flow claim derived from the Insider refs.sys decompilation (the named functions). It is not a fixed on-disk field; on all 66 test images the trash tables were empty, so there is no positive on-disk obligation to probe. Proof is the cited decompiled call chain. The reference documents the Trash Table object (OID 0xD) that the flow uses.
spec.evidence_quote: | 0xD | Trash Table | 0xe0d0 | Deferred deletion queue | CONFIRMED |

spec.desc: VBR+0x03 is the 8-byte FS-name slot holding the ASCII signature "ReFS\0\0\0\0" on normal volumes; BitLocker substitutes the '-FVE-FS-' marker here when the volume is encrypted.
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x03 | 8 | File system name | ASCII | `"ReFS\0\0\0\0"` | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: The claim asserts that VBR+0x03 is the FS-name slot whose normal content ("ReFS") is replaced by the FVE marker only when BitLocker-encrypted. structure_reference.md line 16 fixes offset 0x03 = ASCII "ReFS\0\0\0\0" (CONFIRMED), and RefsIsBootSectorOurs / parse_vbr both read "ReFS" at +0x03 (bs[3:7]==b"ReFS"). vbr_int(0x03,4,"ReFS") tests the first 4 ASCII bytes 'R'(0x52)'e'(0x65)'F'(0x46)'S'(0x53), little-endian u32 = 0x53466552 = 1397113682. PASS on every non-encrypted image proves the field is the ReFS-signature slot the FVE marker is said to replace. The win11refs4gbitlocked image is the single scoped exception where bytes at 0x03 are '-FVE' (0x4556462d) instead — that conditional FVE-presence half is recorded separately in needs_probe (no menu probe tests a presence-vs-replacement on one image).

spec.desc: refsutil fixboot zeroes VBR fields 0x2A-0x57, preserving only geometry
spec.evidence_quote: **Warning:** `refsutil fixboot` is destructive: it zeroes the container size, volume serial, checksum algorithm selector, Extended GUID, and volume flags fields.
spec.explanation: BEHAVIORAL claim about refsutil.exe (a tool action), explicitly 'not in refs.sys driver' (raw_notes). It is not an on-disk invariant of normal ReFS volumes — it only holds on a post-fixboot artifact image. No structural disk probe applies; the proof is the documented fixboot test + reference line 90. cite is the correct mapping (no on-disk obligation across the corpus).

spec.desc: refsutil fixboot resets checksum selector 0x2A from 0x02 to 0x00, mismatching CRC64 metadata
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x2A | 2 | Checksum algorithm selector | u16 | See Subtable A.1b | ENRICHED |
spec.explanation: BEHAVIORAL claim about refsutil fixboot mutating VBR 0x2A (2->0); 'not in refs.sys driver' (raw_notes). It is a before/after tool behavior on one artifact image, not a corpus on-disk invariant. No structural probe over the image corpus can test a tool transition; cite (documented fixboot test) is the correct mapping. Offset 0x2A confirmed by reference Subtable A.1b.

spec.desc: Version evolution narrative: 3.7/3.9 == 3.4 structurally; 3.10 is the transition; CHKP flag 0x2000 is insider-only
spec.evidence_quote: Observed composite values: 0x002 (v3.4/v3.7/v3.9), 0x082 (v3.10), 0x682 (v3.14 native), 0x602 (v3.14 upgraded), 0x07b2 (v3.14 dedup), 0x2682 (Insider).
spec.explanation: This is a multi-part cross-version SUMMARY claim (several independent sub-assertions about 3.7/3.9/3.10/3.14/Insider) spanning many fields, not a single offset/value. No single menu probe can express the whole narrative; the CHKP 0x2000=Insider sub-part is corroborated by the composite CHKP-flags reference line. Mapped to cite as a literature/summary; its testable atoms belong to other per-field specs.

spec.desc: In 224-byte Container Table rows the physical start LCN is at value offset 0xD0 (= row[len-16])
spec.evidence_quote: #### Value — 224-byte row (64 KiB clusters or SHA-256) ... Physical start at offset 0xD0. **Universal parsing rule:** CPC always at value+0x18; physical start always at value[len-16].
spec.explanation: The asserted field (physical start LCN at 0xD0) is a VARIABLE value differing per container/image; there is no constant to assert, and no menu probe tests 'a field exists at offset 0xD0'. cpc only checks clusters-per-container, not the 0xD0 placement, so it does NOT prove this claim. Placed in needs_probe with a layout/consistency shape (physical_start = container_map start, plausible LCN within volume). Probe array here is a placeholder.

spec.desc: OID chronology: generation counter at Object Table value offset 0x18 = checkpoint virtual clock at create/update
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x18 | 4 | Generation counter | u32 | Checkpoint virtual clock at creation/last modification | ENRICHED |
spec.explanation: Mostly a SUMMARY/analysis claim (OID density 76.6%, 128-bit File ID composition, 'stronger chronology than NTFS'). The one offset atom (generation counter at value+0x18) is a VARIABLE clock value per object, with no constant to assert; no menu probe tests this composition. Mapped to cite (analysis report ra_step4_20). The 0x18 offset itself is confirmed by reference line 371.

spec.desc: Two SUPB backup copies near partition end (~total_clusters-14339 and -14338)
spec.evidence_quote: There are **3 copies**: primary at **LCN 0x1E** plus two backups at **VolSize−2** and **VolSize−3** (computed from volume size, `CmsVolume::ReadSuperBlock` @211169).
spec.explanation: The asserted backup LCNs are VARIABLE (a function of volume size, and the reference now states VolSize-2/VolSize-3 rather than the older -14339/-14338 fixed numbers — an apparent discrepancy to resolve). No menu probe reads SUPB at a computed end-of-volume LCN; page_const reads the OT-root page, not the backup SUPBs, so it does NOT prove this claim. Placed in needs_probe (locate backups at the volume-size-derived LCNs and verify 'SUPB' signature + matching GUID/chkp refs). Probe array here is a placeholder.

spec.desc: Page header field 0x40-0x50 is the table identifier (high+low); on the Object Table root page the table-id low at +0x48 equals 0x02.
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x48 | 8 | Table identifier (low) | u64 | MSB+ only | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: GN_PAGE_007 asserts 0x40-0x50 holds the page's table identifier. The reference splits it: 0x40 = 'Table OID (high) + Schema (low)', 0x48 = 'Table identifier (low)'. Root #0 is the Object Table (Table ID 0x02 per Subtable A.4b line 201: '| 0 | Object ID Table | 0x02 | 0xe030 ...'). page_const(0x48,2) reads le32 at +0x48 on the OT root MSB+ page and tests it equals 0x02; it FAILs if the field is not the table identifier or not 0x02, proving the table-identifier field location/value. Constant is image-independent because root[0] is always the Object Table (ID 0x02).

spec.desc: Integrity-stream extents use variable-stride entries: 32B checksum-page entries (flags 0x1c00d0) vs 24B data-run entries (flags 0x180040)
spec.evidence_quote: On-disk layout observation; not directly verifiable from driver code
spec.explanation: Detailed data-run layout claim (entry strides and flag values 0x1c00d0 / 0x180040). The structure_reference.md VBR/SUPB/CHKP/page/page-ref/CT/OT sections I am authoring against do not fix these integrity-extent flag constants, so I cannot verify an offset/value here without guessing. No VBR/CHKP/page menu probe covers data-run flags. Mapped to cite (validated in refs_dataruns.py against the setintegritystreams images); a dedicated extent-flag probe is flagged in needs_probe.

spec.desc: $SI ExternalFileId_2/3 (0x60/0x68) populated only for cross-volume ops; zero on all native v3.7+
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x60 | 8 | ExternalFileId\_2 | u64 | Cross-volume origin ID. Zero on all native v3.7+ images; non-zero only on upgraded v3.4→v3.14 volumes (carryover from v3.4 format). | CORRECTED |
spec.explanation: On NATIVE v3.7+ volumes ExternalFileId_2 at $SI+0x60 is zero on every user object (reference Win11 extension line). Scoped to native v3.7+, si_zero(0x60,8) is exactly the right ABSENCE probe and is non-tautological (a non-zero would FAIL). CAVEAT: it must NOT be applied to upgraded v3.4->v3.14 or to v3.4 images (where the field is legitimately non-zero) — those need a scoped_exception; the harness 'native' grouping must exclude upgraded images. Flagged in needs_probe for the upgraded-image carve-out and for ExtFid_3 at 0x68.

spec.desc: Page header offset 0x00 is the 4-byte ASCII page signature (SUPB/CHKP/MSB+); on a B+-tree root page it reads 'MSB+'.
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x00 | 4 | Signature | ASCII | `"SUPB"`, `"CHKP"`, or `"MSB+"` | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: GN_PAGE_001 asserts the page-header signature lives at +0x00 and is a 4-byte ASCII tag. The page_const probe targets the Object Table root, which is a B+-tree (MSB+) page, so its signature dword must equal 'MSB+' = little-endian u32 0x2B42534D = 725766989 (bytes M,S,B,+). page_const(0,725766989) reads le32 at +0x00 and tests equality; it FAILs on any page whose first dword is not 'MSB+', proving the signature field's offset and value. (The probe also internally guards on pg[:4]==b'MSB+', but here we assert the value explicitly so it is not a tautology.)

spec.desc: Page header Table OID at +0x48 stores the owning table's OID; on the Object Table root page it equals 0x02.
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x48 | 8 | Table identifier (low) | u64 | MSB+ only | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: GN_PAGE_RA_002 asserts the MSB+ page header at +0x48 carries the owning table's OID (e.g. 0x02 = Object ID Table). The probe reads the Object Table root (root[0]), whose owning table is the Object ID Table = Table ID 0x02 (Subtable A.4b line 201). page_const(0x48,2) reads le32 at +0x48 and tests equality with 0x02, FAILing if the page-header owning-table-OID field is absent or not 0x02 — proving the claim on a real on-disk root page. Image-independent: root[0] is always the Object Table.

spec.desc: Page reference offset 0x22 holds a 1-byte checksum-type code (0x02 = CRC64-ECMA on standard CRC64 metadata).
spec.evidence_quote: | 0x22 | 1 | Checksum type | u8 | See Subtable D.1a | CONFIRMED |
spec.explanation: Claim: page-reference +0x22-0x23 is the checksum type (1=CRC32-C, 2=CRC64-ECMA). structure_reference.md D.1 fixes a 1-byte 'Checksum type' at 0x22, and Subtable D.1a enumerates 0x00=None, 0x01=CRC32-C, 0x02=CRC64-ECMA. On a CRC64 volume the page references in the OT-root page header region carry type 0x02. page_const(0x22, 2) tests this exact enum value at the exact offset; a SHA-256 or None volume would not be 0x02, so applicability is scoped to checksum=CRC64. This is a non-tautological enum test. NOTE: page_const reads the page-HEADER at the OT-root page, whereas this field lives in a PAGE-REFERENCE struct (child pointer / CHKP root pointer). If the harness's page_const offset base is the page header rather than a page-reference, this probe is mis-targeted — see needs_probe entry for the precise page-reference-scoped shape.
